mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate anchors front-end rates and supports risk appetite, which steepens term premium expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days when policy is still near the effective lower bound.
regime_hint: policy rate near the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25%, or if the sign flips negative in more than 60% of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS indicates improving nominal spending and growth expectations, which lift real-rate and term-premium expectations and cause the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after at least one negative monthly reading
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS rebounds are followed by flat or lower 10-year yields in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if average 20-day yield change is negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS signals labor-market deterioration, which increases recession odds and drives a flight-to-quality bid into Treasuries, lowering the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS declines
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration trigger does not precede lower 10-year yields in at least 55% of cases, or if the average 20-day yield response is positive after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in INDPRO after a contraction phase signals reopening and tighter capacity utilization, which raises expected real rates and inflation compensation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month after at least two prior monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebounds are not followed by a positive 10-year yield change within 20 trading days in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS stays near zero, real-rate repression and inflation expectations rise together, forcing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days as markets price eventual policy normalization.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is positive year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-plus-low-rate condition does not produce higher 10-year yields on average, or if negative yield changes occur in more than 60% of triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A falling HOUST series after a prior downturn signals housing stabilization and stronger cyclical demand, which lifts growth expectations and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward yield changes are not positive when HOUST stabilizes after a decline, or if the sign is negative in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market breakdown, a rising UNRATE and contracting PAYEMS jointly increase recession probability and compress inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS falls for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if the labor-deterioration condition fails to precede lower 10-year yields in more than 55% of cases, or if average 5-day yield changes are nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: During a deep industrial slump, a turn in INDPRO from contraction to expansion combined with rising PAYEMS signals a durable cyclical bottom, which raises real-rate expectations and makes the 10-year Treasury yield rise over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not precede higher 10-year yields over 60 trading days in at least 60% of instances, or if the average response is flat or negative.
